citation: Amy E. (2005). Oro-facial injuries in Central American and Caribbean sports games: a 20-year experience. Dental Traumatology.
injury_type_raw: acute/emergency orofacial conditions
rate_denominator_raw: (%) of 279 dental-clinic visits = 18 acute orofacial cases
extraction_notes: 20-year retrospective of Central American + Caribbean Sports Games dental services for the Puerto Rican delegation. 2107 total participants across 6 delegations. 279 (13.2%) seen for dental conditions. 18 (6% of 279, 0.85% of all participants) acute orofacial. Lip contusion most frequent (n=4). Basketball had most injuries (n=3). Note: international elite multi-age games; some delegations include youth.

citation: Azadani, E. N., Peng, J., Townsend, J. A., & Collins, C. L. (2023). Traumatic dental injuries in high school athletes in the United States of America from 2005 to 2020. Dental Traumatology, 39(1), 73-81.
injury_type_raw: dental injuries (unspecified type)
extraction_notes: 15-year aggregate. 459 dental injuries / 108,574 total injuries = 0.4% of all athlete injuries. mouthguard_use_rate is *at time of injury* (24.9% wearing MG when injured), not population-level prevalence. Mechanism: 60.4% contact with another player, 31.9% with apparatus.

citation: Azadani, E. N., Peng, J., Townsend, J. A., & Collins, C. L. (2023). Traumatic dental injuries in high school athletes in the United States of America from 2005 to 2020. Dental Traumatology, 39(1), 73-81.
injury_type_raw: dental injuries (unspecified type)
extraction_notes: Competition-only subset: 256 of 459 dental injuries (55.8%). RR vs practice = 3.6 (95% CI 3.0-4.4). AE denominator for competition not in abstract — needs full text.

citation: Azadani, E. N., Peng, J., Townsend, J. A., & Collins, C. L. (2023). Traumatic dental injuries in high school athletes in the United States of America from 2005 to 2020. Dental Traumatology, 39(1), 73-81.
injury_type_raw: dental injuries (unspecified type)
extraction_notes: Practice-only subset: 200 of 459 dental injuries (43.6%). 3 injuries (0.7%) had unclear exposure context. AE denominator for practice not in abstract — needs full text.

citation: Azadani, E. N., Peng, J., Townsend, J. A., & Collins, C. L. (2023). Traumatic dental injuries in high school athletes in the United States of America from 2005 to 2020. Dental Traumatology, 39(1), 73-81.
injury_type_raw: dental injuries (unspecified type)
extraction_notes: Highest per-sport rate among boys' sports. Per-sport AE denominator and injury count require full text. Note: collins2016 (same surveillance, 2008-2014 subset) reported boys' basketball = 2.6 per 100k AE — the rate is broadly stable across the longer window.

citation: Azadani, E. N., Peng, J., Townsend, J. A., & Collins, C. L. (2023). Traumatic dental injuries in high school athletes in the United States of America from 2005 to 2020. Dental Traumatology, 39(1), 73-81.
injury_type_raw: dental injuries (unspecified type)
extraction_notes: Highest per-sport rate among girls' sports. Per-sport AE denominator and injury count require full text.

citation: Cetinbaş T, Yildirim G, Sönmez H. (2008). The relationship between sports activities and permanent incisor crown fractures in a group of school children aged 7-9 and 11-13 in Ankara, Turkey. Dental Traumatology.
injury_type_raw: permanent maxillary/mandibular incisor crown fracture (any cause)
extraction_notes: 2570 students, 10 primary schools, 5 Ankara municipalities. 14.14% of incisal fractures were sport-related. 84% maxillary central incisors. Males higher than females in older group (p<0.01). Bicycling caused significantly higher crown fractures than other sports (p<0.05).

citation: Cetinbaş T, Yildirim G, Sönmez H. (2008). The relationship between sports activities and permanent incisor crown fractures in a group of school children aged 7-9 and 11-13 in Ankara, Turkey. Dental Traumatology.
sport_raw: sport-related subset of incisor crown fractures
injury_type_raw: permanent incisor crown fracture caused by sport (14.14% of 222 fractures)
extraction_notes: 14.14% of the 222 fractured incisors attributed to sport activities (~31 of 222).

citation: Chan YM, Williams S, Davidson LE, Drummond BK. (2011). Orofacial and dental trauma of young children in Dunedin, New Zealand. Dental Traumatology.
sport_raw: multiple — sport one of several causes (collisions, falls, sports)
injury_type_raw: orofacial trauma episodes (288 children, 300 incidents)
extraction_notes: University Dental School clinic Dunedin. Ages 0-10. 86.6% had cause noted. In younger children mainly falls; in school-aged children sports + collisions + falls. Predominant dental injuries: concussions and subluxations (more common in primary dentition, p<0.001). Upper central incisors most affected. Lip injuries dominant non-dental.

citation: Collins, C. L., McKenzie, L. B., Ferketich, A. K., Andridge, R., & Xiang, H. (2016). Dental injuries sustained by high school athletes in the United States, from 2008/2009 through 2013/2014 academic years. Dental Traumatology, 32(2), 121–127.
injury_type_raw: dental injuries (unspecified type)
extraction_notes: Aggregate row across all reported sports. Mouthguard use rate is computed from abstract: 72.5% of injured athletes were not wearing a MG, so 27.5% were — note this is MG use AT TIME OF INJURY (not population prevalence). 95.9% of worn MGs were self-fitted. Per-sport rates by competition vs practice not extracted at aggregate level (see other rows).

citation: Collins, C. L., McKenzie, L. B., Ferketich, A. K., Andridge, R., & Xiang, H. (2016). Dental injuries sustained by high school athletes in the United States, from 2008/2009 through 2013/2014 academic years. Dental Traumatology, 32(2), 121–127.
injury_type_raw: dental injuries (unspecified type)
extraction_notes: Rate during competition only. Abstract reports RR competition vs practice = 3.1 (95% CI 2.3–4.0).

citation: Collins, C. L., McKenzie, L. B., Ferketich, A. K., Andridge, R., & Xiang, H. (2016). Dental injuries sustained by high school athletes in the United States, from 2008/2009 through 2013/2014 academic years. Dental Traumatology, 32(2), 121–127.

injury_type_raw: chipping or fracture of teeth

citation: Hamdan TR, Alshayeb L, Dashash M. (2026). Prevalence and risk factors of orofacial trauma among child and adolescent athletes in Damascus, Syria: a cross-sectional study. BMC Public Health.
injury_type_raw: orofacial dental trauma (self-reported + clinical exam, WHO criteria)
extraction_notes: 11 sports clubs Damascus, multistage cluster sampling. Contact sports (football, basketball) had higher rates than gymnastics/karate. MG use significantly associated with reduced ODT risk (OR=0.286, 95% CI 0.141-0.577, p<0.004).

citation: Labella, C. R., Smith, B. W., & Sigurdsson, A. (2002). Effect of mouthguards on dental injuries and concussions in college basketball. Medicine and Science in Sports & Exercise, 34(1), 41–44.
injury_type_raw: dental injuries
extraction_notes: Subgroup: athletes NOT using custom-fitted mouthguards. AE = 62,273 (= 70,936 total − 8,663 MG users). Injury count not directly in abstract; back-calc from rate × AE ≈ 42 injuries. Confirm with full text. Abstract reports P<0.05 for MG vs non-MG dental injury rate difference.

citation: Labella, C. R., Smith, B. W., & Sigurdsson, A. (2002). Effect of mouthguards on dental injuries and concussions in college basketball. Medicine and Science in Sports & Exercise, 34(1), 41–44.
injury_type_raw: dental injuries
extraction_notes: Subgroup: athletes using custom-fitted mouthguards. AE = 8663 of 70,936 total (12.2% of AE). Injury count not directly in abstract; back-calc from rate × AE = ~1 injury. Confirm with full text.

citation: Levin L, Friedlander LD, Geiger SB. (2003). Dental and oral trauma and mouthguard use during sport activities in Israel. Dental Traumatology.
sport_raw: multi-sport (aggregate, 850 active in at least one sport)
injury_type_raw: dental and oral injuries during sport
rate_denominator_raw: (%) of participants with any oral/dental injury
extraction_notes: 943 young adults (95% male, 5% female), ages 18-19, Israel. 850 (90%) active in >=1 sport. 27% had injuries (229 total); 9% had dental injuries (73). MG aware 27%; MG used 3%.

citation: Levin L, Friedlander LD, Geiger SB. (2003). Dental and oral trauma and mouthguard use during sport activities in Israel. Dental Traumatology.
injury_type_raw: dental and oral injuries from basketball
rate_denominator_raw: (%) of basketball players injured (~96 of total sample played basketball)
extraction_notes: Basketball-attributable: 42% of all sport-related injuries; 7.2% of basketball players reported injury.

citation: Levin L, Friedlander LD, Geiger SB. (2003). Dental and oral trauma and mouthguard use during sport activities in Israel. Dental Traumatology.
injury_type_raw: dental and oral injuries from soccer
extraction_notes: Soccer-attributable: 41% of all sport-related injuries; 6.6% of soccer players reported injury.

citation: Levin L, Samorodnitzky GR, Schwartz-Arad D, Geiger SB. (2007). Dental and oral trauma during childhood and adolescence in Israel: occurrence, causes, and outcomes. Dental Traumatology.
injury_type_raw: dental injuries (lifetime recall)
rate_denominator_raw: (%) of 427 young adults with dental injury
extraction_notes: 427 young adults aged 18-21, lifetime recall. 31.1% had oral/dental injuries (133 total); 72 (16.9%) dental. Causes of dental injuries: falls 64%, sport 23.2%, fights 7.2%, MVA 5.6%. Laceration 47.3% / fracture 41.9%. MG aware 22.6%; MG used 2.8%.

citation: Quarrie, K., Gianotti, S., & Murphy, I. (2020). Injury Risk in New Zealand Rugby Union: A Nationwide Study of Injury Insurance Claims from 2005 to 2017. Sports Medicine, 50(2), 415-428.
injury_type_raw: dental injuries (subset of all injury claims)
extraction_notes: 13-year aggregate across all ages 5-40. Dental = 2.0% of 635,657 total injury claims = 12,713 dental claims (computed). Per-age claim rates (probability of at least one claim per season) are reported in age-stratified rows below; this row is the topline.

citation: Quarrie, K., Gianotti, S., & Murphy, I. (2020). Injury Risk in New Zealand Rugby Union: A Nationwide Study of Injury Insurance Claims from 2005 to 2017. Sports Medicine, 50(2), 415-428.
injury_type_raw: all rugby injury claims (any body site, dental is 2.0% overall)
rate_denominator_raw: probability (%) of at least one injury claim per season (any body site, not dental-specific)
extraction_notes: Age band 5-6 years. This row's rate is the probability of making at least one rugby injury claim per season (any body site), not the dental-specific rate. Dental fraction (2.0% topline) is likely lower in <13 yo (mostly soft-tissue) and higher in adolescents/adults; abstract doesn't stratify dental by age. Demographics: ~10% of players are female; female claim rate is 0.57x male overall.
